claim: SQLite with better-sqlite3 v12.8.0 is the correct primary store for T28 v1. Five independent findings files converge on this choice without contradiction.
better-sqlite3 v12.8.0 bundles SQLite 3.51.3, which closes a critical WAL corruption bug present in 3.51.0–3.51.2.

SQLite has been validated in production at 49,000 nodes with sub-1ms queries (codebase-memory-mcp). T28's projected 10,950 nodes over three years is orders of magnitude below any performance ceiling. The earlier '2.1M nodes / 4.9M edges' figure was marked UNVERIFIABLE — no primary source confirmed it.
correctedFrom: 2.1M nodes/4.9M edges — no citable source found

claim: Write latency for better-sqlite3 is approximately 18µs (53,000 ops/s). node:sqlite is approximately 24µs (41,000 ops/s).
DuckDB has a structural 400–900µs per-row write penalty versus SQLite and should be used as an analytical overlay only, never as primary store.
LibSQL/Turso has known Windows errors (issue #1797) and is not recommended for T28.
LadybugDB v0.15.3 is a community fork of Kuzu (archived October 2025 after Apple acquisition). It is HIGH RISK for v2 upgrade planning due to frozen upstream and three competing unconsolidated forks.

The correct LadybugDB npm package name is `lbug`. Both `@ladybug/core` and `@ladybugdb/core` are wrong. The project has three contradictory installation instructions — a documentation culture signal predicting instability.
LadybugDB's Windows NAPI binding has not been verified on Windows 11 + Node.js v22. The WASM fallback (lbug-wasm) exists but adds worker-thread overhead.
CozoDB is stalled since December 2023 and is HIGH risk as a primary backend for T28.

The official @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory should not be used for T28. It has confirmed data corruption on concurrent sessions (GitHub #2577), no tag support, O(N) search, and degrades unusably beyond 2,000–3,000 entities in Node.js.

n-r-w/knowledgegraph-mcp should not be used as a live dependency. The maintainer has publicly stated disillusionment and last committed November 2025 (not December 2024 as previously cited). It can be used as a reference for API design patterns only.

No single existing MCP server has been confirmed to provide all three of T28's required capabilities: flat #tag API, per-node confidence, and contradiction detection simultaneously. Neuromcp (glama.ai) was not investigated and may provide all three — a 30-minute audit is required before finalizing build-vs-adopt decision.

Building a thin custom MCP layer over direct SQLite with 5-8 tools is the lowest-risk v1 path for T28, avoiding all current server-maintenance risks. Confidence downgraded to MEDIUM-HIGH pending Neuromcp capability audit (OQ-16).

Files are canonical for SourceNodes and human-curated data. Agent-inferred edges (SUPERSEDES, RELATED_TO, ALSO_SEEN_IN) have no parent file and are graph-canonical — stored in a supplemental edges file (.research/graph-edges.jsonl) that is git-tracked and serves as their canonical source. Seven independent production systems confirm the files-canonical invariant for human-facing data.
correctedFrom: 'files canonical' without hybrid boundary — all 7 confirming systems are human-facing tools

claim: The SQLite database can be deleted at any time and rebuilt from .research/ files plus graph-edges.jsonl under one second at T28's scale. No backup strategy is needed for the graph layer.
Every graph node must have a provenance trace to a file artifact via an EXTRACTED_FROM edge. No graph-only data is permitted. Agent-inferred edges use graph-edges.jsonl as their file artifact.
The correct T28 schema uses two node types: SourceNode (all 28 source types via discriminator) and KnowledgeNode (extracted claims and insights).

A-MEM's 7-field node pattern is {content, timestamp, keywords, tags, contextual_description, embedding, links}. The 7th field is `links` (L_i — linked_memories), which is the graph link list for LINKS_TO edges. The fields `valid_at`/`invalid_at` cited previously are Graphiti edge fields, not A-MEM fields.
correctedFrom: fields included valid_at/invalid_at — those are Graphiti edge fields

claim: T28 should use four edge types: LINKS_TO (semantic), CITES (knowledge→source provenance), MENTIONS (source contains claim), SUPERSEDES (version replacement). All edges include valid_at and invalid_at for soft invalidation. Agent-inferred edges (SUPERSEDES, RELATED_TO, ALSO_SEEN_IN) are stored in supplemental edges file.
Flat tags should use an M2M junction table (tags + node_tags), not JSON arrays. Willison benchmark at 100K rows: single tag 1.41ms, AND(2 tags) 2.26ms. JSON array alternative is 40-55ms.
Keywords (specific retrieval signals, stored inline) and tags (broad categorical classifiers, stored in junction table) are distinct and both necessary. Conflating them degrades retrieval quality.

Per-node confidence belongs in a separate node_metadata table using Pattern C propagation: synthesis_confidence = base_confidence * min(1.0, source_count / 3). Full confidence requires 3+ independent corroborating sources.
Soft invalidation (setting invalid_at timestamp) should be used for contradictions and supersession. Never delete nodes or edges. Query current state: WHERE invalid_at IS NULL.
Graphiti's bi-temporal edge model should be adopted event-time half only (valid_at/invalid_at). The system-time audit axis (T') adds complexity without clear T28 v1 value.
Every node should have a stable UUID that survives database resets, decoupled from display title and file path. The t28:// URL scheme provides stable references.
FTS5 with porter unicode61 tokenizer, prefix='2 3', detail='full', and column-weighted BM25 (title 10x, keywords 5x, content 1x) is the correct full-text search configuration.
Three-layer hybrid search (FTS5 + MiniLM 384d vectors + RRF k=60) runs sub-3ms even on a Raspberry Pi Zero and is architecturally justified from day one at T28's scale.
v1 should ship with FTS5-only search to maintain the solo-developer complexity budget of 4-5 new concepts. Hybrid search is a v2 addition gated on FTS5 proving insufficient.

MiniLM-L6-v2 via @huggingface/transformers (v4) is the correct embedding model: 22MB ONNX, 384 dimensions, ~14.7ms per 1K tokens on CPU, no Python, local, no API calls, Windows-confirmed. @xenova/transformers is the deprecated predecessor — do not use.

RRF k=60 is the validated standard for balanced multi-method retrieval fusion. k=1 (Graphiti) is intentionally aggressive for their top-position use case, not appropriate for T28's discovery use case.
Tag pre-filter using junction table CTE should precede FTS5 and vector search. Tag selectivity (1-5% of nodes) is higher than confidence filters and narrows the candidate set most aggressively.
Graph expansion (BFS 1-2 hops after hybrid search returns top-K) provides 18.5% accuracy gain and 90% token reduction (115K → 1.6K tokens) per Zep benchmark.
Context injection should be capped at 500 tokens per query to enable prompt caching. Mem0 benchmark: 26K tokens + 17 seconds vs 6% accuracy loss + 91% lower latency with selective retrieval.
SQLite recursive CTEs handle BFS traversal adequately to ~5,000 nodes for 2-3 hops. Degradation occurs on 5+ hops or dense multi-path graphs, not on raw node count.
Louvain community detection via graphology should be added when the graph reaches 500-1,000 nodes (~3-6 months), providing emergent cluster discovery not possible via traversal.

graphology (TypeScript-native, MIT, 1,600+ stars, approximately 246 dependents, zero native deps) covers Louvain, betweenness, PageRank, BFS, and connected components over the same SQLite store. Risk is LOW-MEDIUM on ecosystem size; justified on technical fit.
correctedFrom: 4,900+ dependents — actual is ~246; own sub-packages were incorrectly counted

claim: Louvain community detection performance: ~53ms at 1,000 nodes, ~938ms at 50,000 nodes. Full graph reload from SQLite required each run (not incremental).

Betweenness centrality (O(E*V)) is structurally analogous (not clinically identical) to bridge centrality in addiction/mental health network research. T28's domain has structural similarities to comorbidity network graphs where bridge nodes are high-value. Run periodically as background job, cache results, never compute per-query.
MAGMA 4-graph decomposition achieves LoCoMo 0.700 vs A-MEM 0.580 but carries substantial operational complexity (4 edge sets, vector DB, async consolidation). Defer to v3+.

The migration scope from existing .research/ files is: 18 SourceNodes, 167 KnowledgeNodes (skip 1 decision:skip entry), 167+ EXTRACTED_FROM edges, plus RELATED_TO, SUPPORTED_BY, and MEMBER_OF edges. Runs in a single transaction under one second.
9 of 13 repository slugs in extraction-journal.jsonl are non-derivable algorithmically from the source field. A manually authored source-slug-map.json is required before migration can run.
The migration script should follow the scripts/debt/intake-manual.js pattern. All required helpers (generate-content-hash.js, normalize-file-path.js, sanitize-error.js) already exist in the project.

Analysis.json files have three schema variants: Gen A (v4.0-4.2), Gen B (v4.3), and Website (v1.0), plus an AWS variant with additional meta wrapper. All six normalization differences fit in ~50 lines of code.

Seven post-migration validation queries (node counts, edge counts, orphan checks, PRAGMA integrity_check, PRAGMA foreign_key_check) cover all integrity cases. Rollback is delete .db + re-run migrate.js.

ALSO_SEEN_IN edges (KnowledgeNode→KnowledgeNode cross-source dedup) should be deferred post-migration. Cross-source dedup requires semantic comparison across sources and is a graph operation, not a migration step.
Content-hash comparison (SHA-256 or XXH3) should be used for incremental sync, not mtime. Two-phase: (1) cleanup orphans, (2) scan with hash comparison. All operations in SAVEPOINT transactions.
The .research/*.db, .research/*.db-wal, and .research/*.db-shm files must be added to .gitignore. They are currently absent from the project's .gitignore.
PRAGMA WAL mode, NORMAL synchronous, foreign_keys ON, and temp_store MEMORY should be set on every connection open. Do not use synchronous=OFF (data loss on crash).

Three-stage dedup funnel: (1) exact/normalized match at absorb, (2) iText2KG weighted cosine (0.6 name + 0.4 label, threshold start at 0.7 — iText2KG default) at absorb, (3) LLM resolver for 0.65-threshold band at checkpoint. Mandatory calibration after 167-node migration: manually review top-20 pairs in 0.65-0.78 band before locking threshold.
correctedFrom: threshold 0.78 — no source; iText2KG default is 0.7

claim: iText2KG weighted embedding (0.6 name + 0.4 label) prevents homonym false merges. Single embedding without weighting produces false positives at Stage 2.
Dedup merge strategy: >= 0.90 cosine → physical merge (surviving node inherits max confidence), 0.65-0.90 → SAME_AS edge (both preserved, deferred for /reorganize), < 0.65 → no action.
Checkpoint cycle should fire every 15 absorbs or manually via /graph-checkpoint. At T28's pace of ~10 sources/day, this fires approximately every 1-2 days.
Node split trigger: >= 3 distinct edge-type clusters AND >= 8 total observations. Do not auto-split — present candidates to user. Thresholds from farzaa's heuristic, no published validation.
Staleness threshold for edge invalidation: confidence < 0.3 AND last_seen > 180 days AND no corroborating sources. More conservative than A-MEM's 90-day threshold (unverified).
Orphan detection threshold: > 5% of total nodes triggers cleanup. After batch deletions with freelist_count > 20% of pages: run PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) then VACUUM.

Subgraph serialization as triples for Claude provides 90% token reduction (Zep: 115K → 1.6K tokens). Protocol: 2-hop neighborhood → serialize as triples → prompt Claude with triples + 'Answer using ONLY the knowledge graph. Cite SPECIFIC EDGES.'

Contradiction detection requires either CONTRADICTS edges (created at absorb time — depends on detection pipeline), vector cosine similarity with LLM judgment, or 2-hop subgraph serialization to Claude. Pure SQL cannot detect semantic contradiction.
The MCP layer should be stateless. MCP tools call a t28-core library — they do not own connections, embed text, or run queries directly. No LLM reasoning should be inside the storage layer.
LLM proposes mutations (structured proposal with KEEP/UPDATE/MERGE/SUPERSEDE), deterministic executor validates schema and applies. LLM never directly writes to SQLite or mutates files.

A-MEM retrieve-then-judge pattern: on ingest of a new KnowledgeNode, retrieve top-k similar existing nodes, LLM judges whether to link and update neighbor contextual_description. 'Bidirectional' means any existing node CAN be updated, not that updates are symmetric.
Chunk at semantic boundaries (headings, paragraphs), not byte count. The Obsidian retriever found byte-count chunking produces poor FTS5 matches for structured content.
Graphiti+Zep requires Python runtime, external LLM API on every write, and Neo4j or Docker. This is over-engineered for T28 v1 and should be skipped.

LadybugDB has fragmented into at least three community forks (LadybugDB, Vela-Engineering multi-writer, Bighorn/Kineviz). Fork consolidation is unresolved. Kuzu upstream is archived (Apple acquisition Oct 2025). This makes LadybugDB HIGH risk for v1 or v2 adoption.
sqlite-vec v0.1.9 is pre-v1 with a solo maintainer. Windows .dll binding stability has not been benchmarked. Pin to v0.1.9 and defer until v2, verifying Windows binding before committing.
BasicMemory is AGPL v3. Its architecture patterns are safe to adopt. Its code cannot be copied for network-served productization. Local CLI/MCP use is safe.

SQLite has no bus factor concern. LadybugDB has a single named project leader, unconfirmed funding, a 6-month fork age, and frozen upstream (Kuzu archived). SQLite's 30+ year track record is a significant risk advantage.

Graphiti confidence/fact_rating API is NOT in open-source graphiti-core. Source code inspection finds no confidence_score field. T28 must implement confidence as a custom Pydantic attribute in the attributes dict.

DuckDB + sqlite_scanner can attach a SQLite file read-only and run aggregations 30-50x faster than SQLite without schema changes. Verify locking behavior before recommending as v2+ analytical overlay.

The Dendron anti-pattern: building general intelligence infrastructure without an anchored user workflow has no PMF regardless of technical quality. T28 should build the graph as a subsystem serving specific agent workflows, not a general-purpose intelligence layer.

The v1 schema must include the 384d embedding column with NULL values from day one. Populate in v2 when adding MiniLM. No ALTER TABLE needed at v2 transition. SQLite ALTER TABLE limitations make pre-wiring the column in v1 DDL the correct approach.

Agent-inferred edges (SUPERSEDES, RELATED_TO, ALSO_SEEN_IN, LINKS_TO between KnowledgeNodes) must be stored in a supplemental edges file (.research/graph-edges.jsonl) that is git-tracked. This file serves as their canonical source and is read by t28 rebuild-graph. Without this, agent-inferred edges would be graph-only data violating the files-canonical invariant.

A 30-session query pattern audit should be performed before schema finalization. If 80%+ of real queries are simple FTS5 keyword lookups, the bi-temporal model and junction tables may be over-engineering. This is a 2-hour exercise.
